Australian Tamil Broadcasting Corporation (ATBC) is a not-for-profit radio station broadcasting programs primarily in Tamil language 24 hours a day. ATBC operates its’ studios in Sydney, Melbourne and Toronto, Canada.
Tamil people have migrated to Australia from Sri Lanka, India, Singapore, Malaysia, South Africa and Fiji. There are approximately 50,000 first and second generation Tamils living in the Sydney and Melbourne Metropolitan areas and there are more than 85,000 Tamis live Australia wide.Community broadcasting in Australia is the voice of the community. Australian Tamil Broadcasting Corporation (ATBC) was established to provide Australian Tamils with Information services, which are not adequately served by the existing mainstream media. ATBC was established as a non-profit entity with active participation from the Tamil community. listen Guru Granth Sahib ji on a daily basis. Guru Granth Sahib ji containing 1430 Ang (Pages). If a Sikh regular reading Gurbani it could complete within 72 hours. So we are distributing this Gurbani into a 30 minute audio. The listener can easily listen and enjoy the Gurbani.Completion of Gurbani takes 161 days around. We will put daily next Ang (Pages) audio. Now the question is why people like to hear it. Because this is very hard to save 161 audio collection in mobile or Bluetooth player, etc. Because it is hard to remember which Ang(page) Gurbani we were listening. Using online radio our team keep the record of everyday so daily listeners can enjoy Gurbani without any repetition.Listen Gurbani radio station online.
